title: Beam management for interference mitigation for wireless networks
abstract: A technique of reducing interference at a wireless node includes: determining a location of a base station relative to a wireless node, determining, based at least on the location of the base station relative to the wireless node, a beam-reduction region that includes one or more interfering transmit beams of the base station within the beam-reduction region, and causing the base station to decrease a transmit power for the one or more interfering transmit beams within the beam-reduction region.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method of reducing interference at a wireless node comprising: determining a location of a base station relative to a wireless node; determining, based at least on the location of the base station relative to the wireless node, a beam-reduction region that includes one or more interfering transmit beams of the base station within the beam-reduction region; causing the base station to decrease a transmit power for the one or more interfering transmit beams within the beam-reduction region. 2. The method of claim 1 and further comprising: determining an orientation of an antenna for a base station; and determining an orientation of an antenna of the wireless node; and wherein the determining a beam-reduction region comprises: determining, based on the location of the base station relative to the wireless node and the orientations of the base station antenna and the wireless node antenna, a beam-reduction region that includes one or more interfering transmit beams of the base station within the beam-reduction region. 3. The method of any of claims 1 -2 wherein the wireless node comprises a backhaul node, and wherein causing the base station to decrease a transmit power for the one or more interfering transmit beams reduces, at the backhaul node, interference between a signal transmitted by the base station and another signal received by the backhaul node. 4. The method of any of claims 1 -3 wherein the base station comprises a 5G base station or access point. 5. The method of any of claims 2-4 wherein the causing the base station to decrease a transmit power for the one or more interfering transmit beams comprises: causing the base station to decrease a transmit power for each of one or more transmit beams for each of a plurality of BSs, wherein an amount of decrease in transmit power for each transmit beam is based on the location of the base station relative to the 

 wireless node and the orientations of the base station antenna and the wireless node antenna. 6. The method of any of claims 1 -5 wherein the causing the base station to decrease a transmit power for the one or more interfering transmit beams comprises: causing the base station to turn off the one or more interfering transmit beams, including either ceasing to transmit a signal via the one or more interfering beams, or transmitting a signal at zero power via the one or more transmit beams. 7. The method of any of claims 2-6 wherein the determining an orientation of an antenna for the base station comprises: determining an orientation of an antenna for a base station, including an azimuth angle and an elevation angle of the antenna for the base station. 8. The method of any of claims 2-7 wherein the determining an orientation of an antenna for the wireless node comprises: determining an orientation of an antenna of a wireless node, including an elevation angle and an azimuth angle of the antenna of the wireless node. 9. The method of any of claims 1 -8 wherein the determining a beam-reduction region comprises determining a beam-reduction region as an overlap of the following two regions: a first region in which a transmit beam transmitted by the base station within this first region would create interference at the wireless node; and a steerable region for base station transmit beams within one or more sectors of the base station. 10. The method of any of claims 1 -9 wherein the determining a beam-reduction region comprises determining a beam-reduction region as an overlap of the following two regions: an off-axis region within a range of an axis between the base station and the wireless node in which a transmit beam transmitted by the base station within this off-axis region would create interference at the wireless node; and 

 a steering region of a sector of the base station that defines a steerable region of a plurality of transmit beams of a sector of the base station. 11. The method of any of claims 1 -10 wherein the determining a beam-reduction region comprises: determining a beam-reduction region as an overlap of the following two regions: an off-axis region between a) a line identifying an axis between the base station and the wireless node and b) a maximum off-axis angle based on a receive beam width of the wireless node; and a steering region of a sector of the base station, including a region between the antenna orientation for a sector of the base station and a maximum steerable range of the antenna for the sector. 12. The method of any of claims 1 -1 1 and further comprising: receiving, from a probe user device located in close proximity or co-located with the wireless node, signal measurements for one or more of the interfering beams within the beam-reduction zone; and wherein the causing comprises causing, based on the signal measurements for one or more of the interfering beams, the base station to decrease a transmit power for the one or more interfering transmit beams within the beam-reduction zone. 13. The method of any of claims 1 -12 wherein the determining a location of a base station relative to a wireless node comprises at least one of the following: determining, based on a table lookup, a location of the base station relative to the wireless node; receiving, from a probe user device located in close proximity or co-located with the wireless node, information indicating a location of a base station relative to a wireless node; and receiving, from a probe user device located in close proximity or co-located with the wireless node, signal measurements for one or more of the interfering beams within the beam-reduction zone. 14. An apparatus comprising means for performing a method of any of claims 1 -13. 15. An apparatus comprising at least one processor and at least one memory including computer instructions, when executed by the at least one processor, cause the apparatus to perform the method of any of claims 1 -13. 16. A computer program product, the computer program product comprising a computer-readable storage medium and storing executable code that, when executed by at least one data processing apparatus, is configured to cause the at least one data processing apparatus to perform the method of any of claims 1 -13. 17. An apparatus comprising at least one processor and at least one memory including computer instructions, when executed by the at least one processor, cause the apparatus to: determine a location of a base station relative to a wireless node; determine, based at least on the location of the base station relative to the wireless node, a beam-reduction region that includes one or more interfering transmit beams of the base station within the beam-reduction region; and cause the base station to decrease a transmit power for the one or more interfering transmit beams within the beam-reduction region. 18. A method of reducing interference at a wireless node comprising: receiving, for each of a plurality of beams transmitted by a base station, a beam identification and a signal measurement, measured by a probe user device in close proximity to or co-located with a wireless node, for the beam; determining that one or more of the beams are interfering beams having a signal measurement greater than a threshold; and causing the base station to decrease a transmit power for the one or more interfering transmit beams so as to reduce interference at the wireless node from the base station. 19. The method of claim 18 wherein one or more antenna characteristics of the probe user device match one or more antenna characteristics of the wireless node. 20. The method of any of claims 18-19 wherein the probe user device and the wireless node are connected to the same antenna. 21. The method of any of claims 18-20 wherein the causing comprises: performing, by the base station, a beam-specific power control for one or more of the beams to reduce a signal measurement of one or more of the beams of the base station, as measured by the probe user device, to be less than a threshold. 22. The method of any of any of claims 18-21 wherein the wireless node comprises a backhaul node, and wherein causing the base station to decrease a transmit power for the one or more interfering transmit beams reduces, at the backhaul node, interference between a signal transmitted by the base station and another signal received by the backhaul node. 23. The method of any of any of claims 18-22 wherein the base station comprises a 5G base station or access point. 24. The method of any of claims 18-23 wherein the causing the base station to decrease a transmit power for the one or more interfering transmit beams comprises: causing the base station to turn off the one or more interfering transmit beams, including either ceasing to transmit a signal via the one or more interfering beams, or transmitting a signal at zero power via the one or more transmit beams. 25. An apparatus comprising at least one processor and at least one memory including computer instructions, when executed by the at least one processor, cause the apparatus to: receive, for each of a plurality of beams transmitted by a base station, a beam identification and a signal measurement, measured by a probe user device in close proximity to or co-located with a wireless node, for the beam; determine that one or more of the beams are interfering beams having a signal measurement greater than a threshold; and cause the base station to decrease a transmit power for the one or more interfering transmit beams so as to reduce interference at the wireless node from the base station. 26. A computer program product, the computer program product comprising a computer-readable storage medium and storing executable code that, when executed 

 by at least one data processing apparatus, is configured to cause the at least one data processing apparatus to perform a method of any of claims 18-24. 27, An apparatus comprising at least one processor and at least one memory including computer instructions, when executed by the at least one processor, cause the apparatus to perform the method of claims 18-24. 28. An apparatus comprising means for performing a method of any of claims 18-24.
